commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=135983cebfdb73fa084f3f0fdf4841b6f0362359)
commands.review_summary:
The PR migrates session naming toward a canonical `title` field across gateway schemas, session metadata, create/list/patch/resolve, `/name`, Control UI display, reserved plugin slot keys, and focused tests while mirroring legacy `label`.

PR surface: Source +68, Tests -1. Total +67 across 17 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding by source inspection: nullable `sessions.patch` clears are accepted by the protocol schema, but PR head normalizes the values before checking for null. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review remains needed because the branch still has a blocking nullable-clear regression, compatibility-sensitive naming semantics, and missing contributor real behavior proof.

target.title: fix(sessions): migrate session naming to canonical title
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Session naming was partly migrated from legacy `label` to canonical user-editable `title`, but the runtime contract was incomplete. New gateway schemas accepted `title` while `sessions.create` dropped it, `sessions.list` did not project or filter it, and the durable `SessionEntry` type did not declare the persisted field. That left new title-based clients and older label-based clients able to disagree about the same session name.

## Why This Change Was Made

This completes the title contract while preserving the legacy label mirror:

- `sessions.create` now passes `title` into the shared patch applier, which mirrors it to `label` for compatibility.
- `SessionEntry` now declares `title?: string` as durable session metadata.
- Gateway session rows now project normalized `title` from `title` or legacy `label`.
- `sessions.list({ title })` now filters by the same normalized title comparison used by patch and resolve.
- Session list search includes the canonical title field.
- Focused tests cover `/name`, create, patch, resolve, list projection/filtering, and legacy label compatibility.

## User Impact

Users and clients can set, clear, list, create, patch, and resolve session names through the canonical `title` field without breaking existing `label` consumers. Legacy label-only session entries still appear as titled rows and remain resolvable through title-based lookup.

## Evidence

Head SHA: `135983cebf`

### After-fix: lint gate passes on PR head

The previous head (`6d05bb66ac`) failed `check-lint` with:
```
src/plugins/session-entry-slot-keys.ts(141,15): error TS2344: Type '"title"' does not satisfy the constraint 'never'.
```

This was fixed by adding `"title"` to `SESSION_ENTRY_RESERVED_SLOT_KEY_LIST` in `src/plugins/session-entry-slot-keys.ts`, completing the compile-time guard that every `SessionEntry` key is excluded from plugin extension slot names.

### After-fix: touched tests pass on PR head

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/commands-name.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-resolve.test.ts src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.create.test.ts
```

Result:

```
Test Files  14 passed (14)
Tests  706 passed (706)
Duration  56.58s

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  7 passed (7)
Duration  34.18s

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 107.95s
```

### ClawSweeper P2 findings addressed

1. **sessions-patch.ts: compare title/label after parsing** — Raw `title` and `label` strings are now normalized with `trim().normalize("NFC")` before comparison, so compatibility clients sending the same logical name with whitespace or NFC differences are no longer incorrectly rejected.

2. **session-display.ts: add title to shared UI row contract** — `GatewaySessionRow` in `ui/src/ui/types.ts` now declares `title?: string`, removing the need for the local `SessionDisplayRow` cast in the display helper. Typed UI consumers now see the canonical title field through the shared contract.

@clawsweeper re-review
target.author: jason-allen-oneal

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
